nodes.label: AI ability to provide hard-to-find information
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable source lacking depth
nodes.grounding_span: if there's a very specific research question I have whose answer might not be easy to find on the first page of a regular search, I might ask a chatbot

nodes.label: AI trustworthiness and verification
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't trust the accuracy as much as I trust myself

nodes.label: AI integration in teaching profession
nodes.label_negative: Human involvement in qualitative and social teaching work
nodes.grounding_span: direct involvement is in the nature of teaching work, which is a qualitative and social activity that probably shouldn't be outsourced

nodes.label: AI as plagiarism risk
nodes.label_negative: AI as legitimate research tool for students
nodes.grounding_span: I had one student who clearly *was* using AI to generate their work

nodes.label: AI freeing time for human-centric work
nodes.label_negative: AI deployment driven by profit undermining human work
nodes.grounding_span: the routinized computer-based tasks would be increasingly automated, so more time would be freed up for irreducibly human tasks like teaching itself

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't trust the accuracy as much as I trust myself

nodes.label: Human-centered engagement
nodes.grounding_span: direct involvement is in the nature of teaching work, which is a qualitative and social activity

nodes.label: Pedagogical integrity and student understanding
nodes.grounding_span: difficult to coax them towards using their own intellect instead

nodes.label: Human flourishing in work
nodes.grounding_span: more time would be freed up for irreducibly human tasks like teaching itself

nodes.label: Cautious skepticism about AI accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: I wouldn't trust the accuracy as much as I trust myself

nodes.label: Resistance to outsourcing teaching to AI
nodes.grounding_span: direct involvement is in the nature of teaching work, which is a qualitative and social activity that probably shouldn't be outsourced

nodes.label: Frustration with student AI misuse
nodes.grounding_span: I had one student who clearly *was* using AI to generate their work. That was frustrating

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I expect to use it more as a research tool as it becomes more reliable over time

nodes.label: Excitement about AI enabling business expansion
nodes.grounding_span: more likely AI will be employed wherever it is profitable, sometimes for better, sometimes for worse

nodes.label: Selective, need-based engagement with AI
nodes.grounding_span: I rarely use AI in my typical workday

nodes.label: Domain-based boundary setting — clear separation of AI-appropriate vs. human-only tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I would consider it irresponsible not to do it myself
